title: Plugin Lifecycle

primary_when: request is explicitly about plugin lifecycle phases
must_not_own_when: request is mainly about patching, UI, or parsing

solves: coordinating mod runtime phases after loader attach
keeping modules, patches, probes, UI, sync, and diagnostics in a predictable order
making startup and teardown inspectable when game state is unstable

core_principles: Lifecycle should be explicit, phase-based, and observable.
Modules should have stable initialization order and isolated failure handling.
Shutdown should run in reverse ownership order where practical.
Runtime state should degrade when a module fails instead of corrupting unrelated systems.
Game lifecycle events should be converted into stable internal events and snapshots.
constraints: Do not let one module failure prevent diagnostics from reporting the failure.
Do not hide startup order inside attribute discovery without a deterministic priority rule.
Do not perform game-object mutation before the relevant game lifecycle state is ready.
Do not keep stale references to Unity objects after scene or car lifecycle changes.
Do not require feature modules to know raw hook timing.
decision_rules: Use plugin-lifecycle as support whenever loader-integration starts more than one runtime subsystem.
Use it as primary when the request is about phase design, module ordering, shutdown, or runtime readiness.
If game state appears through hooks, publish lifecycle events before feature modules consume it.
If a subsystem depends on a fragile hook or probe, gate it behind availability diagnostics.
If runtime work can occur off the Unity main thread, route it through an explicit dispatcher.

preferred_patterns: module registry with deterministic priority and state tracking
event bus for game lifecycle transitions
snapshot/facade layer over raw game objects
explicit main-thread dispatcher and fixed-step policy
avoid_patterns: module startup driven by incidental static constructor order
feature modules subscribing directly to Harmony patch methods
stale game object references without lost/reset events
unbounded per-frame work in lifecycle callbacks

recipes.name: Stabilize plugin runtime lifecycle
recipes.when: Use when a mod grows from one feature into a toolkit with modules, hooks, probes, UI, sync, and diagnostics.
steps: Define runtime phases: configure, attach, initialize modules, update, fixed update, GUI, shutdown.
Register modules with explicit priorities and track created, initialized, stopped, and faulted states.
Initialize diagnostics and logging before fragile integrations.
Convert game hook callbacks into internal events and lifecycle snapshots.
Gate feature modules on readiness and integration availability.
Capture shutdown errors without hiding earlier initialization failures.

recipes.name: Gate a feature on fragile game integration
recipes.when: Use when a feature depends on a hook, reflected member, game context, or multiplayer transport that may be unavailable.
steps: Name the required hook, probe, lifecycle state, or transport dependency.
Expose its readiness through diagnostics or a lifecycle snapshot.
Prevent the feature from mutating game state until readiness is true.
Log a clear degraded reason when the feature is disabled.
Resume or reinitialize only through an explicit lifecycle event.

pitfalls: Treating loader Awake or Start as the whole lifecycle model.
Letting feature modules own raw game lifecycle transitions.
Ignoring scene reload, car swap, and object invalidation paths.
Assuming optional integrations are always available.
review_checklist: Are lifecycle phases named and ordered?
Are hook/probe-dependent features gated by readiness?
Are stale game references cleared through lifecycle events?
Does shutdown run deliberately and capture errors?
Can diagnostics explain why a subsystem is unavailable?

references.title: Runtime Integration
references.interpretation: Use this first for runtime phases, module lifecycle, diagnostics, hooks, probes, and facades.
references.why: It captures the practical architecture for a reusable Unity-game modding toolkit.

references.title: Harmony Patching
references.interpretation: Use when plugin lifecycle depends on hook availability or patch state reporting.
references.why: It defines how patch availability should feed runtime lifecycle and diagnostics.

references.title: Reflection Probe Degradation
references.interpretation: Use when lifecycle readiness depends on private or version-sensitive game state.
references.why: It defines how fragile probe availability should be reported and gated.

references.title: Diagnostics And Readiness
references.interpretation: Use when module lifecycle needs health, readiness, degraded-state, or failure-policy guidance.
references.why: It defines the runtime signals that make lifecycle behavior inspectable.
